food_subgroup: Specialty tea-based beverages with dairy foam and botanical additives

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for matcha-coconut-blue spirulina specialty drinks. FOODON_03301737 (tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Tea-based beverages) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for matcha cloud drinks with coconut foam and blue spirulina.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 (Tea drink) is the closest parent. No specific code for matcha-coconut-spirulina specialty beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one 500 ml cup as served by HEYTEA, containing matcha base, coconut milk, blue spirulina, and dairy cream foam with cane sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: Matcha green tea beverage with coconut milk and cream foam, using USDA FoodData Central reference values for matcha tea, coconut milk beverage, and cream foam components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific HEYTEA product. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted analysis: matcha green tea base (~60% by weight, USDA FDC #2003571 matcha powder dissolved in water), coconut milk beverage (~20%, USDA FDC #170172), dairy cream foam (~15%, USDA FDC #1097 heavy cream diluted), and cane sugar syrup (~5%, USDA FDC #169655). Values represent a typical full-sugar preparation. Confidence is low due to proprietary formulation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of matcha green tea and coconut milk beverages — analogous estimate based on: 'International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021' (Atkinson et al.) and University of Sydney GI Database entries for green tea and coconut milk
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HEYTEA Matcha Cloud Coconut Blue. GI estimated at 40 (low) based on: (1) green tea / matcha beverages have negligible GI contribution from tea solids; (2) coconut milk has a low GI (~40, University of Sydney GI Database); (3) added cane sugar raises effective GI; (4) dairy cream foam contributes fat which blunts glycemic response. Weighted composite estimate of GI ~40 (low category). Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (40 × 42.5 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 17.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 8.5 g/100g × 500 g = 42.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~140 mg per 500 ml serving); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 28 mg sodium per 100 g (140 mg per serving), this beverage poses a low sodium burden and is generally compatible with a hypertension-management diet, though individuals should account for total daily sodium intake from all sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content from cane sugar (~39 g sugar per 500 ml serving); significant glycemic load of ~17 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The default full-sugar preparation delivers approximately 39 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of ~17 per serving, which is clinically significant for blood glucose management; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request the no-sugar-added option or reduce sugar level, and monitor postprandial glucose response accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk and dairy cream foam (~14.5 g saturated fat per 500 ml serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contributes approximately 14.5 g of saturated fat primarily from coconut milk and dairy cream foam, which exceeds the recommended daily limit of 20 g for many adults; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this beverage occasionally and consider requesting reduced-cream or dairy-free modifications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Matcha Cloud Coconut Blue Spirulina Drink is a specialty layered beverage sold by HEYTEA (喜茶), a Chinese premium tea chain, at its Marina Bay Sands outlet in Singapore. The drink combines a matcha green tea base with coconut milk, a signature 'cloud' dairy cream foam topping, and blue spirulina for natural blue-green colouring — no artificial colours are used. The beverage is sweetened with real cane sugar by default, though customers may request no-sugar-added or reduced-sugar options. It contains dairy and soy and is not suitable for vegans or those with dairy or soy allergies. Nutritionally, a 500 ml serving provides approximately 360 kcal, 42.5 g carbohydrates (predominantly free sugars), 19 g fat (largely saturated from coconut and cream), and 6 g protein. The glycemic load per serving is approximately 17, placing it in a clinically significant range for blood glucose management. Matcha contributes L-theanine and catechin antioxidants; blue spirulina provides phycocyanin, a natural blue pigment with antioxidant properties. The drink is priced at SGD 9.90 and is positioned as a premium artisanal beverage. Clinically, the high free-sugar and saturated-fat content warrants caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The low sodium content makes it broadly compatible with hypertension management when consumed in moderation.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA, ICMR-NIN) for this specific HEYTEA proprietary product. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted analysis of matcha tea, coconut milk, dairy cream, and cane sugar using USDA FoodData Central reference values. The proprietary formulation, variable sugar levels, and foam-to-liquid ratio introduce meaningful uncertainty. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central reference entries for matcha powder (FDC #2003571), coconut milk beverage (FDC #170172), heavy cream (FDC #1097), and cane sugar (FDC #169655). No direct laboratory analysis or approved-source entry exists for this product. 2. The product description states sugar-free sweetener may be unavailable; default preparation uses cane sugar. Nutrient values reflect the full-sugar default. 3. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used for estimation. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component sources; no hydrogenated fats are listed in the product ingredients. 5. GI is an analogous estimate of 40 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for coconut milk and green tea beverages; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = 17.0 calculated as (GI 40 × 42.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100. 6. Halal status is not confirmed; HEYTEA Singapore does not carry a Muis halal certification as of the data collection date. 7. This product is served at HEYTEA Marina Bay Sands, Singapore, priced at SGD 9.90. 8. The food is classified under Beverages (not Desserts) in this record as it is a liquid drink product despite being listed under Desserts on field survey.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built complete record from scratch. Assigned canonical English name with spirulina specified. Added all five mandatory multilingual names for Singapore cuisine category (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Estimated nutrients via USDA FoodData Central component-weighted analysis. Set GI to 40 (low) via analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Set trans_fat_g to 0.0 with confirmed zero justification. Used only closest_parent ontology match_type values. Achieved PASS on first iteration.
